role: The Palette is the rail region that lets the operator browse the node-type registry and add nodes to the canvas.
capabilities: Renders the palette rail with data-ui-ref="ui://rail/palette"
Lists node types from the AppContext oinfo registry
Groups node types by declared kind (process, content, fallback other)
Orders lanes: process first, content second, then any other kinds alphabetically
Renders each kind lane with a kit LaneHead showing a kind label and item count
Renders each node type as a kit Surface card with kind-tone spine, monogram, name, and add cue
Maps process/content kinds to design-system tones (sig/wire) and labels
Calls addNode(type) when a card is clicked
Shows an EmptyState when the registry has no types
substance_why: It implements complete presentational structure, grouping logic, tone mapping, click-to-add interaction, and empty state using the shared kit components.
interactions_present: Click a node-type Surface card to invoke addNode(t)
Hover/focus styling provided by the kit Surface component
Browser tooltip via the title attribute on each card
interactions_missing: No drag-and-drop from palette onto canvas
No search or filter of node types
No keyboard navigation or shortcuts for palette items
No loading state while oinfo is fetched
No error handling if addNode fails
No favorites, recents, or recently-used ordering
No preview or description of each node type
No disabled state for forward-provision kinds
No scroll virtualization for large registries
performance_risks: Regroups and re-sorts the full oinfo list on every render without memoization
Creates a new inline onClick closure for every card on every render
Renders every registry type as a DOM node with no virtualization
Builds className and title strings per item per render
reuse_why: The component already expresses the new design vocabulary and region responsibility, but it needs search, drag-and-drop, and render memoization to be production-ready.
decomposition_hints: Extract grouping/sorting logic into a useNodeTypesByKind hook or utility
Split PaletteLane into its own component
Split PaletteItem/Surface card into its own component
Add a PaletteSearch or PaletteFilter subcomponent
Move kind tone/label constants into a shared design-system module if reused elsewhere
